summary: Loop-health telemetry evidence is surfaced in readiness, pinned by cross-artifact parity, and separated from execution readiness as a named release blocker when unresolved.

observations.problem: Learning readiness now surfaces loop-health telemetry coverage explicitly, but it still needed a narrow parity guard ensuring mechanism-review session calibration, outcome metrics session rollups, and routing-provenance loop-health evidence stay aligned in the generated readiness baseline.
observations.why_it_matters: Without a focused ledger guard, future upstream shape changes can silently degrade the proposal-to-run-to-outcome-to-readiness evidence chain until a later audit notices that readiness lost or misreported learning evidence.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a targeted generated-baseline parity check that verifies learning_readiness metrics and signals stay synchronized with diagnostics.loop_health_summary, mechanism review session calibration, and outcome metrics session evidence.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-28: generated-report contracts now pin readiness learning metrics and warning signals against the checked-in mechanism review report, outcome metrics report, and routing provenance aggregate. Updated on 2026-04-29: unresolved learning readiness is now a separate schema-backed `release_blockers[]` entry with a named `learning_blocked_by_*` id, `accepted_risk` flag, required evidence, and generated-report parity coverage.
